business.headline: Sensex tumbles 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Sensex and Nifty fall sharply after opening higher as banking and auto stocks drag markets down. Here's what triggered the sudden sell-off and what it means for investors.

Live: IRCTC directs station catering units to use microwaves, inductionLPG Shortage Live Updates: Concerns over a shortage of commercial LPG cylinders have surfaced across several Indian cities, with restaurants and hotels reporting disruptions in gas supply over the past few days. Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, government response, city-wise impact and supply situation across India.

India eases FDI rules for neighbouring countries: What the new 10% rule meansThe decision was approved by the Union Cabinet and the changes are intended to boost foreign investment, help startups access global funding and support manufacturing growth in India.

Why Reliance Industries shares are gaining in early trade todayAt around 9:23 a.m., RIL shares were trading at Rs 1,427.20, up Rs 18.40 or 1.31% from the previous close of Rs 1,408.80. The stock opened at Rs 1,424.90 and touched an intraday high of Rs 1,434.00.

Sensex, Nifty trade flat in early session; Reliance gains, banks weighAt around 9:18 a.m., the BSE Sensex was at 78,279.66, up 73.68 points or 0.09%. The Nifty 50 stood at 24,256.90, down 4.70 points or 0.02% after touching an intraday high of 24,292.35.

Air India fares to rise as Iran war pushes up jet fuel pricesAir India said the fuel surcharge is due to the sharp rise in aviation turbine fuel (ATF) prices since early March 2026, triggered by supply disruptions in the Gulf region.

G7 stops short of releasing oil reserves, asks IEA to assess market optionsAfter soaring to a more than three-year high in the previous session, oil prices plummeted 7 per cent on Tuesday as US President Donald Trump predicted the war in the Middle East could end soon, easing concerns about prolonged disruptions to oil supplies.

India eases FDI rules for China, allows up to 10% stake without govt approvalUnder the revised policy, overseas investors with up to 10% beneficial ownership from countries sharing a land border with India will now be allowed to invest through the automatic route.

Main Hoon Naa: IndiGo interim head's message to employees amid leadership changeRahul Bhatia expressed gratitude to employees who worked tirelessly to restore operations during that challenging period, praising their resilience and dedication.

IndiGo CEO Pieter Elbers steps down 3 months after operational meltdownThe resignation comes after the airline faced its worst-ever operational crisis in December, which drew scrutiny from both the public and the government.

Airlines raise fares as Iran conflict sends jet fuel prices soaringThe Iran-US conflict has disrupted shipping through a key global oil export route, pushing oil prices higher. As a result, airline tickets on some routes have become much more expensive, and there are growing concerns that travel demand could weaken if the situation continues.

No LPG crisis, production up 10% today, says government, warns against fake newsGovernment sources said oil refineries have increased LPG production by around 10% following directions issued earlier and that additional administrative measures have been put in place to prevent hoarding and malpractice.

Middle East countries cut daily oil output amid Hormuz disruptionFour of the region's largest producers, including Saudi Arabia, Iraq, the United Arab Emirates and Kuwait have collectively reduced output by as much as 6.7 million barrels per day.
Sensex closes 640 points higher, Nifty above 24,200; M&M gains 4%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 639.82 points to close at 78,205.98, while the NSE Nifty50 added 233.55 points to end at 24,261.60.

Essential Commodities Act invoked by India amid LPG shortage. What does it mean?Restaurants, hotels and commercial kitchens in cities such as Delhi, Mumbai and Bengaluru have reported difficulty getting commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to scale down operations or temporarily shut. The situation has pushed the government to invoke the Essential Commodities Act

Silver jumps nearly Rs 10,000: Will the Iran-US war push prices even higher?Geopolitical shocks are once again driving money into precious metals. Silver has surged nearly Rs 10,000 amid rising tensions between the US, Israel and Iran. As uncertainty deepens, the big question now is: can silver rally even further?

Prolonged Gulf conflict could be catastrophic for oil markets, says Aramco chiefSpeaking during a media call after the company released its 2025 financial results, Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser said the disruption represents the most serious crisis faced by the region's oil and gas sector in recent years.

EPF rate stays at 8.25%: Should investors continue with VPF or choose SIPs?For many salaried employees, EPF and Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF) are trusted tools for retirement savings. But with the EPF rate staying at 8.25%, some investors are wondering if they should keep contributing to VPF or move extra savings into SIPs for better long-term returns?

Highlights: No need to panic, India expands oil sourcing to 40 countries, say Govt sourcesLPG Shortage Highlights: A growing LPG cylinder shortage is disrupting restaurants and hotels across several Indian cities, raising concerns over a widening gas supply crisis. Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. The shortage is being reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, where restaurant bodies say supplies have become irregular over the past few days. The government has asked oil companies and refineries to increase LPG production to stabilise supply. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, city-wise impact on restaurants, supply disruptions, and steps being taken to address the crisis.

When crude oil hit a record $147 per barrel in 2008 and the reason was not warCrude oil briefly touched $120 amid Gulf tensions, reviving memories of the historic 2008 oil shock when prices hit $147. But unlike this conflict, the 2008 surge was not driven by war or geopolitics. So what pushed oil prices so high? Read on to find out.

entertainment.headline: K-dramas vs reality: Priyanka Mohan, Ra Karthik on filming Made in Korea in Seoul Netflix's Made in Korea, the first Indo-Korean film, blends travel, humour and cultural discovery. Director Ra Karthik and actor Priyanka Mohan spoke to India Today to talk about filming in Seoul and similarities between Tamil and Korean languages.

You cannot dig up any dirt on me: Ridhima Pandit denies dating Shubman Gill In a recent interview, Ridhima Pandit dismissed dating rumours linking her to cricketer Shubman Gill, calling them completely false. The actor added that she considers herself a "non-controversial figure" and insisted that no one can "dig up any dirt" on her.

Ileana D'Cruz captures real motherhood with a note on her 'feral' little toddler Actor Ileana D'Cruz shared a monochrome selfie during a brief break while feeding her newborn, giving fans a glimpse into her life as a mother of two.

Priyanka Chopra opens up on raising daughter Malti away from public attention Priyanka Chopra has spoken about her reasons for keeping her daughter Malti Marie out of the limelight. She cited privacy and safety as her main concerns after a past incident involving a stranger.

Dhurandhar 2 vs Ustaad Bhagat Singh: Superhit rage takes on Pawan Kalyan's stardom Yash's Toxic has been postponed from March 19, but Dhurandhar: The Revenge has now reportedly run into Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh on that date. With the Ranveer Singh-starrer riding hype as few films do and Andhra Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan returning after They Call Him OG, the clash could be the most intriguing one at the Telugu box office in 2026.

Splitsvilla X6's Sorab Bedi reacts to Malaika Arora dating rumours, seeks respect Speculation about Malaika Arora and Sorab Bedi's relationship surfaced after their party pictures went viral. Bedi clarified that they are just friends and expressed concern over social media reactions.

Tina Ahuja speaks on Govinda–Sunita divorce rumours after tough years Tina Ahuja finally addressed the speculation surrounding Govinda and Sunita Ahuja's marriage, admitting the past two years have been turbulent. Here's what she shared.

Anurag Dobhal car crash: Brother says suicide on Live can't turn lies into truth YouTuber Anurag Dobhal's brother, Kalam Ink, has responded to the viral crash livestream and the allegations made against their family. In a series of videos, he denied the claims and said "attempting suicide on Instagram Live" cannot turn "lies into truth."

Did Shekhar Kapur reject Aamir Khan as his assistant? Filmmaker clarifies Filmmaker Shekhar Kapur revealed why he did not hire Aamir Khan as an assistant director. Kapur believes this rejection was fortunate as it led Aamir to become a superstar actor instead.

Is Prabhas set to collaborate with Eko director? New pic sparks buzz Actor Prabhas' recent meeting with Malayalam director Dinjith Ayyathan, who helmed Eko, has sparked fresh speculation about a potential collaboration. Eko was one of the critically acclaimed films of 2025.

RGV says Dhurandhar The Revenge will rattle South cinema if it earns Rs 2,000 crore Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma says Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 could disrupt the dominance of South films if it crosses the ₹1500–2000 crore mark. Here's why the filmmaker believes the sequel could reshape box office trends.

Puneeth Rajkumar's Akash: Why this 20-year-old Appu film still feels special Puneeth Rajkumar's 2005 musical hit film Akash is set to re-release in theatres on March 13. Alongside the late actor, the film stars Ramya and features a diverse music album that remains reminiscent for Kannada audiences to this day.

Rihanna-A$AP Rocky and children were home when woman fired gunshots: Attorney A woman from Florida faces multiple charges, including attempted murder, after allegedly firing a semiautomatic rifle outside the Los Angeles home of musician Rihanna and A$AP Rocky. Authorities said the couple and their children were inside the property at the time of the incident.

Best to end it here: Director Parthiban says he's closing chapter on Trisha row Filmmaker R Parthiban has decided to end the controversy with actor Trisha after his remarks at a Chennai event sparked backlash. He posted a brief apology and shared a detailed video explaining his comments and stance.

Cardi B seen with oxygen mask after Denver show, jokes about city's thin air Videos of Cardi B using an oxygen mask during her concert in Denver caught fans' attention. The rapper later addressed the moment with humour, while experts noted that high altitude can cause shortness of breath and fatigue, especially during intense physical activity.

Neeru Sehgal on playing Kohrra S2's Loveleen: Felt like being allowed to breath Actor Neeru Sehgal talked about moving from advertising to long-format storytelling with her role as Loveleen in Kohrra Season 2. She shared how performing in Punjabi, preparing for the character and working on a Netflix series shaped her experience.

Trisha-Parthiban showdown: Public spat explained in 10 points Actor Trisha and filmmaker R Parthiban are embroiled in a public dispute following the latter's controversial remarks at a Chennai award show. The exchange has drawn widespread attention and is yet to die down.

Critics' Choice Awards 2026 full nominees list: Homebound, Black Warrant take lead The 8th Critics' Choice Awards shared the full list of nominees, with Black Warrant and Homebound leading in their respective categories. The event will take place on March 24 in Mumbai.

Divya Agarwal receives rape threats from Prince Narula fans, shares abusive texts Divya Agarwal claimed she received online rape threats from fans of Prince Narula following their dispute on the show The 50. Sharing video messages, the actor also called Narula "self-obsessed."

general news.headline: Supreme Court allows passive euthanasia for Harish Rana in historic verdictThe court ruled that clinically assisted nutrition qualifies as medical treatment and can be withdrawn if medical boards recommend it, reiterating that the key test is whether continuing treatment serves the patient's best interest.

Iran busts US and Israeli claims on missile launchers, surprises with salvoWith the war in its second week, Iran was firing 90% fewer missiles than immediately after the strikes by the US and Israel on February 28. While the US and Israel peg this as their success in destroying Iran's launchers, Tehran claims fewer missiles is part of its evolving strategy. It proved itself with a barrage of newer missiles on Tuesday.

Strategic patience: Iran continues to keep India close despite US, Israel tiesDespite India's close ties with Israel and the US, Iran continues to maintain a restrained and pragmatic approach towards New Delhi, recognising the strategic value of preserving a long-standing partnership built on energy ties and geopolitical balance.

Tehran's air is cleaner than Delhi despite exploding bombs, raging warThe contrast is striking. Tehran, a city of nearly nine million people and located in a region facing military strikes and energy infrastructure fires, still recorded cleaner air than India's capital.

How Bumrah's unusual action created cricket's most dangerous slower ballsJasprit Bumrah was once again at the centre of the limelight in India's T20 World Cup 2026 win. At India Today, we explore the origin story of the man who coached him in his early days, and why his unorthodox action was never changed.

Justice Pardiwala tears up while delivering Harish Rana euthanasia verdictJustice JB Pardiwala was visibly moved and briefly teared up while delivering a landmark judgment allowing passive euthanasia for Harish Rana, a 32-year-old man who has remained in a persistent vegetative state for 13 years.

Hospitality associations warn that eateries in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata are struggling to procure commercial cylinders, forcing some to cut menus or scale down operations. The government, however, says there is no nationwide LPG crisis and has ordered refineries to increase production, with output raised by about 10% to stabilise supplies. The supply concerns come amid global energy market disruptions linked to tensions in West Asia, which have affected fuel logistics and availability. RGV says Dhurandhar The Revenge will rattle South cinema if it earns Rs 2,000 croreFilmmaker Ram Gopal Varma says Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 could disrupt the dominance of South films if it crosses the ₹1500–2000 crore mark. Here's why the filmmaker believes the sequel could reshape box office trends.

Woman removed from flight for playing video aloud, meltdown caught on cameraA woman was escorted off an American Airlines flight from Miami to Tampa after refusing to use headphones while playing a video on her phone.

Live: Qatar warns of possible Iranian attack, says mediation impossible while under fireUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the joint front of Israel and the United States entered its 12th day on Wednesday, with fresh Iranian missile attacks targeting central Israel before dawn as air raid sirens sounded, and Israeli air defences intercepted incoming rockets. Iranian state media also reported new strikes on US military installations in Bahrain early Wednesday, following a wave of Iranian missile and drone attacks late Tuesday on bases linked to the US in Qatar, Iraq's Kurdistan, the UAE and Bahrain. The escalation comes after intense US and Israeli airstrikes on Iran on Tuesday, described by the Pentagon and witnesses as among the heaviest of the war so far. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned it could block oil shipments from the Gulf unless the attacks stop, raising fears of disruption to global energy supplies. Meanwhile, the White House reiterated US President Donald Trump's warning of severe retaliation if Iran attempts to halt shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, even as he said US forces had destroyed 10 Iranian 'inactive' mine-laying vessels in recent strikes. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East war.

Anurag Dobhal car crash: Brother says suicide on Live can't turn lies into truthYouTuber Anurag Dobhal's brother, Kalam Ink, has responded to the viral crash livestream and the allegations made against their family. In a series of videos, he denied the claims and said "attempting suicide on Instagram Live" cannot turn "lies into truth."

Sensex tumbles nearly 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Sensex and Nifty fall sharply after opening higher as banking and auto stocks drag markets down. Here's what triggered the sudden sell-off and what it means for investors.

Why coffee badging is becoming the new workplace trend among Gen ZAs hybrid work reshapes office culture, employees are redefining what it means to "show up" at work. A new trend called coffee badging where workers briefly visit the office before leaving to work elsewhere is gaining attention across global workplaces.

Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill was great T20 World Cup call by Gambhir: HarbhajanT20 World Cup: Harbhajan Singh praised Gautam Gambhir and the selectors for backing Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill at the T20 World Cup. Kishan repaid that faith with a breakout campaign as India lifted the title.

Trump announces historic $300 billion oil refinery in Texas, thanks RelianceAnnouncing the project in a Truth Social post, Trump said the refinery will be built in Brownsville, Texas, as part of a USD 300 billion deal aimed at boosting American energy production and strengthening economic ties with international partners.

SA, WI travel decisions made on safety: ICC rejects bias claimsT20 World Cup: The ICC on Wednesday, March 11 said that the South Africa and West Indies teams will leave India within the next 36 hours. The international cricket body also rejected claims that they showed any bias towards both sides during the travel fiasco that has seen them get stranded in India.

Dhurandhar 2 vs Ustaad Bhagat Singh: Superhit rage takes on Pawan Kalyan's stardomYash's Toxic has been postponed from March 19, but Dhurandhar: The Revenge has now reportedly run into Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh on that date. With the Ranveer Singh-starrer riding hype as few films do and Andhra Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan returning after They Call Him OG, the clash could be the most intriguing one at the Telugu box office in 2026.

Best interest of patient is paramount: Key takeaways from SC's euthanasia verdictThe Supreme Court, allowing withdrawal of life support for a 32-year-old man who has remained in a vegetative state for 13 years, ruled that continuing treatment with no hope of recovery was not in the patient's best interest.

India's next T20 dynasty: Sanju Samson and Co carry Rohit-Kohli's legacy forwardTwo years after Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Ravindra Jadeja stepped away from T20Is, India's new core delivered the perfect answer. Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan, Axar Patel and Shivam Dube carried the legacy forward with a title-winning run at the 2026 T20 World Cup.

Passive euthanasia allowed for Harish Rana: What are the next steps?Passive euthanasia follows a defined process inside hospitals. After the SC allowed the withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment for Harsh Rana and directed that the patient be admitted to the palliative care centre at All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), doctors begin a transition from aggressive treatment to comfort-focused care

Puneeth Rajkumar's Akash: Why this 20-year-old Appu film still feels specialPuneeth Rajkumar's 2005 musical hit film Akash is set to re-release in theatres on March 13. Alongside the late actor, the film stars Ramya and features a diverse music album that remains reminiscent for Kannada audiences to this day.

India now has over 50 living CheetahsThe birth marks a proud moment for Project Cheetah, which aims to restore the world's fastest land animal to Indian landscapes decades after it went extinct in the country.

Why climate change will empty your tap before it floods your streetClimate on My Plate is India Today Science's new series on how the climate crisis is reshaping the everyday things you eat, drink, buy and own. This week, it is the glass of water you pour without thinking.

US-Iran war reveals why India needs to tap into clean energyThe ongoing conflict might end soon, or it might not. The guarantee of nations working collectively towards a sustainable future is unlikely and, therefore, preparing for the worst is the best step forward.

sports.headline: Virat Kohli's preparations begin, hits the nets ahead IPL 2026: Watch Virat Kohli has been spotted hitting the nets ahead of IPL 2026, with the Indian batting great putting in the hard yards early as defending champions RCB gear up for the new season.

Gambhir responds to MS Dhoni's viral post, explains why he doesn't smile in dugout Gautam Gambhir responded to MS Dhoni's viral smile remark after India's T20 World Cup win and explained why he chooses to keep a serious face during matches. Dhoni's special message to Gambhir after the T20 World Cup win went viral.

India's next T20 dynasty: Sanju Samson and Co carry Rohit-Kohli's legacy forward Two years after Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli and Ravindra Jadeja stepped away from T20Is, India's new core delivered the perfect answer. Sanju Samson, Ishan Kishan, Axar Patel and Shivam Dube carried the legacy forward with a title-winning run at the 2026 T20 World Cup.

PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi stranded in Qatar amidst ongoing West Asia crisis PSG president Nasser Al-Khelaifi has been stranded in Qatar for over a week due to West Asia war flight disruptions, as the club prepares to host Chelsea in the Champions League.

Sen in Zen mode: Lakshya swaps the sprint for a steady, mature ascent Lakshya Sen's All England 2026 final appearance was bittersweet, yet it highlighted the evolution of his game and mindset. After the pain of Paris Olympics disappointment, the 24-year-old has shifted into a calmer, Zen-like phase, prioritising recovery and mental strength over the relentless grind for ranking points.

Jasprit Bumrah to focus more on ODIs after T20 World Cup triumph Jasprit Bumrah is set to prioritise ODIs over T20Is as India begin planning for the 2027 World Cup. The BCCI and team management will carefully manage his workload in the upcoming cycle.

How Bumrah's unusual action created cricket's most dangerous slower balls Jasprit Bumrah was once again at the centre of the limelight in India's T20 World Cup 2026 win. At India Today, we explore the origin story of the man who coached him in his early days, and why his unorthodox action was never changed.

Gambhir slams Kirti Azad after T20 World Cup remark: Degrading your own players India coach Gautam Gambhir slammed Kirti Azad's comment about the team taking the T20 World Cup trophy to the Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad, saying it was degrading the players and their achievement on the day.

7 Iranian women footballers receive asylum in Australia during West Asia tensions Seven members of Iran's women's soccer team were granted humanitarian visas in Australia after seeking asylum, while the rest of the squad returned home following protests and concerns over safety amid the Iran war.

T20 World Cup medal in hand, Shivam Dube reveals how he returned home via train T20 World Cup: Just hours after helping India win the T20 World Cup, Shivam Dube quietly boarded a train from Ahmedabad to Mumbai. With flights full, the all-rounder chose the fastest route home to see his children.

Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill was great T20 World Cup call by Gambhir: Harbhajan T20 World Cup: Harbhajan Singh praised Gautam Gambhir and the selectors for backing Ishan Kishan over Shubman Gill at the T20 World Cup. Kishan repaid that faith with a breakout campaign as India lifted the title.

SA, WI travel decisions made on safety: ICC rejects bias claimsT20 World Cup: The ICC on Wednesday, March 11 said that the South Africa and West Indies teams will leave India within the next 36 hours. The international cricket body also rejected claims that they showed any bias towards both sides during the travel fiasco that has seen them get stranded in India.

FC Barcelona and Liverpool stunned on UCL return, Atletico thrash clueless SpursBarcelona and Liverpool stumbled on hostile away nights in the Champions League Round of 16 first legs. Atletico Madrid punished Tottenham brutally, while Bayern Munich delivered a ruthless six-goal statement against Atalanta.

ODI World Cup, WTC wins bigger than T20 World Cup: Manjrekar odd take goes viralFormer cricketer Sanjay Manjrekar has gone viral over his odd take on the T20 World Cup. Manjrekar feels that ODI World Cup and the WTC wins are bigger achievements than winning the T20 World Cup.

England right-back Kyle Walker retires 4 games short of 100 international capsKyle Walker has announced his retirement from international football, bringing the curtain down on a 14-year England career. Walker played for England 96 times and appeared at five major tournaments.

Frustrated with ICC, Windies board arranges flights for stranded playersThe West Indies Cricket Board is arranging commercial flights for its players and staff who continue to be stranded in India. The CWI has taken matter into its own hands after repeated delays by the ICC.

Rishabh Pant turns to Yuvraj Singh for career-revival in IPLLucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant is training under Yuvraj Singh ahead of the IPL 2026. Pant, who had a horrid time in the IPL last year, is hoping to revive his career in the T20 format.

Bangladesh vs Pakistan ODI series Live Streaming: Squads, all you need to knowBAN vs PAK: Shaheen Shah Afridi's Pakistan and Mehidy Hasan Miraz's Bangladesh are all set to lock horns in a three-match ODI series to be held from March 11 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur.

Australian GP Review: Dominant Mercedes, familiar Ferrari frustrationsThe 2026 Formula 1 season began with a thrilling Australian Grand Prix, but beneath Mercedes' dominant 1-2 finish were deeper questions about the new regulations, Ferrari's strategy woes, and a promising new rookie generation.

Watch: Sanju Samson's emotional reunion with family after dream T20 World CupT20 World Cup: Sanju Samson shared an emotional reunion with his family in Trivandrum, thanking his parents and brothers for their unwavering support throughout his cricketing journey.

Why visit only temples? Harbhajan responds to Kirti Azad's dig at Team IndiaFormer cricketer Harbhajan Singh has responded to 1983 World Cup winner Kirti Azad's question - why do the Indian team always visit a temple, and not a mosque, or a church? Harbhajan's response has gone viral.

Babar Azam's future in the dark? Pakistan focus on youngsters before ODI World CupBAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi stated that Pakistan rested Babar Azam for the Bangladesh series to provide opportunities to youngsters, with next year's ODI World Cup in mind.

New Zealand's moment in the sun not far awayT20 World Cup: The New Zealand men's team has played four ICC limited-overs finals since 2000 but has ended up on the losing side each time. On Sunday, the Kiwis were defeated by India by 96 runs in a one-sided final at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad.

Manisha Kalyan's world-class free-kick breathes life into Indian footballWomen's Asia Cup 2026: Manisha Kalyan's stunning free-kick gave India a brief moment of hope, but Chinese Taipei ran out 3-1 winners in Sydney, ending India's Women's Asia Cup campaign without a single point.

Sahibzada Farhan set for ODI debut after T20 WC surge, confirms Shaheen AfridiBAN vs PAK, 1st ODI: Shaheen Shah Afridi confirmed that Sahibzada Farhan, Shamyl Hussain, and Maaz Sadaqat will make their ODI debuts against Bangladesh in the series opener on Wednesday in Mirpur.

Watch: Ishan Kishan deflects question on Kirti Azad's controversial temple remarksIshan Kishan calmly avoided commenting on Kirti Azad's controversial remarks about taking the T20 World Cup trophy to a temple after India's victory. Instead, he urged journalists to focus on India's triumph and the team's memorable campaign.

Arshdeep fined for heated T20 World Cup final bust-up with Daryl MitchellFast bowler Arshdeep Singh has been reprimanded for his heated bust-up with New Zealand's Daryl Mitchell. In the T20 World Cup final, Arshdeep chucked a ball straight into the batter's body, in the 11th over of the game.

IPL 2026 schedule for first 20 matches to be announced by March 13: BCCIThe BCCI will unveil the schedule for the first 20 days of IPL 2026 by March 13, as confirmed by Secretary Devajit Sikia. The board is releasing fixtures in phases while factoring in state election dates.

Reminiscent of Viv Richards: Gavaskar's glowing verdict on Samson's World Cup heroicsT20 World Cup 2026: Sunil Gavaskar compared Sanju Samson to Viv Richards and Mahela Jayawardene after his Player of the Tournament performance at the T20 World Cup, praising his rare purity of batting.

Hell of a career: Starc pays special tribute to Alyssa Healy on Australia farewellMitchell Starc paid tribute to his wife Alyssa Healy after her international farewell, celebrating her remarkable career following Australia's Day-Night Test win over India at the WACA in Perth.

Delhi Capitals head coach Hemang Badani finds new role in The HundredHemang Badani has been appointed head coach of Southern Brave for the 2026 season of The Hundred, with Ian Bell serving as his assistant. The Brave will start their campaign on July 22 against Welsh Fire in Southampton.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
